stem: A hot air balloon is tethered to the ground by a 60-foot cable. The cable makes a 60-degree angle with the horizontal ground. Given that sin 60° = √3/2, cos 60° = 1/2, and tan 60° = √3, what is the height of the balloon above the ground, in feet?
explanation: The cable (60 feet) is the hypotenuse of the right triangle, and the balloon height is the side opposite the 60° angle. Using sine: sin 60° = opposite/hypotenuse = height/60. Therefore, height = 60 × sin 60° = 60 × (√3/2) = 30√3 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Used cos instead of sin, finding the horizontal distance instead of the height: 60 × cos 60° = 60 × (1/2) = 30.
distractorRationale.C: Set up the sine ratio inverted, dividing by sin instead of multiplying: 60 ÷ (√3/2) = 60 × (2/√3) = 120/√3 = 40√3.
distractorRationale.D: Used tan instead of sin, treating the hypotenuse as the adjacent side: 60 × tan 60° = 60 × √3 = 60√3.

imageDescription: A right triangle with the hypotenuse labeled as a 60 ft cable running from a point on the ground up to a hot air balloon. The angle between the cable and the horizontal ground is labeled 60°. The vertical leg represents the balloon height and is labeled with a question mark. The horizontal leg represents the distance along the ground from the tether point to directly below the balloon.
